characters.name: A'Karonys
characters.summary: A short, round fire-wielding High Mage whose burning staff is taller than a spear. During the battle with Moon's Spawn, he unleashes fire before being frozen and crushed.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Ammanas
characters.summary: A hooded shadow-associated figure from Chapter One, later named in connection with Cotillion and Shadow's designs. Through Chapter Four, his overlap with Shadowthrone feels strongly suggested but should remain treated as known-so-far uncertainty.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Anomander Rake
characters.summary: Named as the Lord of Moon's Spawn and Lord of the black-skinned Tiste Andii. He appears at a distance on the floating fortress, long silver hair blowing, and unleashes devastating Elder magic.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Aragan
characters.summary: An officer managing events in Gerrom after the slaughter. He questions a new soldier named Sorry and records her attachment to the Bridgeburners under High Fist Dujek Onearm.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Bellurdan
characters.summary: A Thelomen High Mage and lifelong companion of Nightchill. After Pale he kneels beside her remains, horribly burned and grieving, while insisting Tayschrenn has always protected the Empire's dream.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Caladan Brood
characters.summary: A warlord mentioned as commanding Tiste Andii forces alongside the Crimson Guard against the Malazan 5th Army. He is associated with old epic poetry, but does not appear directly in the chapter.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Calot
characters.summary: A thin, century-old mage in Tattersail's cadre and her intimate companion. He jokes through fear before the assault on Moon's Spawn, then dies defending Tattersail from a blast during the battle.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Cotillion
characters.summary: A shadow-associated assassin figure linked to the fishergirl in Chapter One and named again around Paran's murder. Chapter Four makes the connection between Cotillion, Shadow, and Sorry feel much more dangerous, though still not fully explained.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Dancer
characters.summary: A figure from Tattersail's memory, linked to the night before Mock's throat was opened and before an unnamed future Emperor stole into her master's Hold. No direct scene details are shown in this chapter.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Dujek Onearm
characters.summary: High Fist of the 2nd Army and commander of Onearm's Host. In Chapter Four he confirms Paran's assignment, voices the pressure to merge/disband forces, and privately gives Whiskeyjack permission to walk if the Darujhistan mission somehow succeeds.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Felisin
characters.summary: A name attached to the chapter's opening verse before the Pale sequence. No scene details are provided here, so identity and relevance remain unclear at this boundary.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Fiddler
characters.summary: A Bridgeburner sapper with a strong instinct for danger. Chapter Four makes him more visible: scarred from the tunnels, uneasy around Tattersail, and repeatedly sensing that something bad is about to break loose.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Fisher Keltath
characters.summary: Named as the author of the epic poem 'Anomandaris', which Tattersail recalls during the discussion of Caladan Brood and Anomander Rake. He does not appear directly.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Fishergirl's father
characters.summary: The fishergirl's unnamed father, described as poor and missing an arm. He depends on her return and the repair of fishing nets.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Ganoes Paran
characters.summary: A young noble officer from House Paran, now sent by Adjunct Lorn to command Whiskeyjack's squad and stay close to Sorry. In Pale he is murdered by a shadow-linked assailant, held from death by Oponn's meddling, and later wounds the Hound Gear with his sword.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Garnet
characters.summary: A guard at House Paran in Unta who admits Ganoes Paran and answers his questions with dry familiarity.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Hairlock
characters.summary: A former 2nd Army mage now bound into a small wooden puppet body. He is using chaotic paths between Warrens to hunt whoever set up the Bridgeburners, but his mind is fraying and he is becoming dangerous even to Tattersail and Quick Ben.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Hedge
characters.summary: A Bridgeburner sapper and card-player at Knobb's Inn. By Chapter Four he is present around Paran's recovery and is described by Mallet as the sort who adopts stray dogs and needy creatures.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Ilgrand Lender
characters.summary: A person mentioned by the fishergirl in connection with debt or repayment after her father's fishing gear is lost. Not directly seen in the chapter.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Kalam
characters.summary: A large Seven Cities Bridgeburner and former Claw by implication. He works closely with Quick Ben, suspects Sorry, helps hide Paran after the attack, and is treated by the squad as the blade to Quick Ben's mind.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Laseen
characters.summary: Empress of the Malazan Empire. Chapters Three and Four deepen the sense that her court may be moving against Dujek, Whiskeyjack, the Bridgeburners, and anyone still tied to the old Emperor's memory.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Lorn
characters.summary: Adjunct to Empress Laseen and Paran's handler. Though offstage in Chapters Three and Four, her mission hangs over Paran: he is to join Whiskeyjack's squad, stay close to Sorry, and wait for the moment to act.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Mallet
characters.summary: The Bridgeburners' healer. In Chapter Four he treats Captain Paran and explains that something has already healed the flesh while leaving the deeper shock unresolved.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Mock
characters.summary: A figure from Tattersail's memory whose throat was opened on a past night connected to Dancer and the man who would later rule an Empire. No direct appearance occurs in this chapter.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Nedurian
characters.summary: The former commander of Tattersail's cadre, mentioned as having died at Mott Wood before Dujek chose Tattersail to command. He does not appear directly.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Nightchill
characters.summary: One of the three High Mages chosen for the attack on Moon's Spawn. She is described as raven-haired, tall, imperious, and cruel; she is later torn apart by a demon during the battle.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Quick Ben
characters.summary: A tall, thin Seven Cities mage in Whiskeyjack's squad, far more powerful and knowledgeable than he lets on. He guides Hairlock through dangerous Warren work, suspects Sorry is tied to Shadow, and openly frightens Tattersail with the depth of his sorcery.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Rigga
characters.summary: An old wax-witch/seer traveling with the fishergirl. She speaks bitterly of the Empire, gives a prophecy about the girl, and is killed during the violence on the road.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Rusty
characters.summary: A sergeant in the Seventh Regiment mentioned by Tattersail when she recognizes a marine at Dujek's command tent. He is not directly seen in the chapter.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Sorry
characters.summary: Original Chapter One state: a young fishergirl from Itko Kan caught beside the road during imperial violence. After Rigga's death, shadow-associated powers take an interest in her; this entry preserves the first identity before the later Bridgeburner recruit state.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Tattersail
characters.summary: A surviving 2nd Army sorceress at Pale. By Chapter Four she is grieving, furious at Tayschrenn, cooperating uneasily with the Bridgeburners, guarding Hairlock, and newly caught in the clash between Oponn, Shadow, and the Hounds.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Tavore Paran
characters.summary: Ganoes Paran's younger sister. She appears at House Paran in Unta and notes that she thought Ganoes was in Itko Kan.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Tayschrenn
characters.summary: Laseen's High Mage at Pale. Through Chapter Four he remains a central suspect in the deaths at Pale, asks Tattersail for a Deck reading, cautiously hunts the strange Warren disturbances, and stands uneasily above Dujek and the 2nd Army.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Topper
characters.summary: The Claw Master, a tall half-blood Tiste Andii in green, who finds Paran by Warren at sea and delivers Lorn's orders about Sorry, Whiskeyjack's squad, and Darujhistan.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Trotts
characters.summary: A Barghast Bridgeburner in the Ninth, tattooed and scarred, seen dealing cards with ponderous patience at Knobb's Inn before the squad leaves for Darujhistan.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Unnamed Itko Kan captain
characters.summary: A local captain working under Lorn at the massacre site. He has served in Itko Kan for years, reports on the dead, and is visibly uneasy around the Adjunct.
characters.imageStyle: WoW cinematic / Arcane-adjacent painterly field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Whiskeyjack
characters.summary: Sergeant of the Ninth Squad of the Bridgeburners. Through Chapter Four he is trying to keep his battered squad alive, distrusts the mission to Darujhistan, defends a human explanation for Sorry against darker theories, and remains deeply tied to Dujek and the old army.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Sorry — Bridgeburner recruit
characters.summary: The later state of the fishergirl now serving with Whiskeyjack's squad. She has been with them for two years, still called a recruit, moves under a hooded raincloak with a small satchel, and feels wrong to the squad: young in appearance, but not young in the eyes.
characters.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache / restrained WoW cinematic field-guide portrait, spoiler-safe through Chapter Four

characters.name: Toc the Younger
characters.summary: A scarred, half-burned Claw/Messenger Corps outrider in the 2nd Army. He escorts Paran into Pale, warns him about the Bridgeburners and the unstable army politics, and offers himself as a contact if trouble comes.

characters.name: Picker
characters.summary: A blunt Bridgeburner corporal first seen lounging in the barracks. She sizes Paran up, predicts grim odds for him, and later finds his bleeding body in the alley and calls for a healer when he unexpectedly screams back to life.

characters.name: Antsy
characters.summary: A red-faced Bridgeburner sergeant with a huge handlebar moustache. He loses money to Hedge, knows where Whiskeyjack has gone, and helps respond after Paran is found wounded.

characters.name: Shadowthrone
characters.summary: King of High House Shadow as named in Paran's near-death encounter. He appears as a shadow-presence with Hounds at Hood's Gate and decides it may be better to follow Paran than finish him there.

characters.name: Oponn
characters.summary: The Twins of Chance: a pale young man and a blonde young woman who meddle with Paran's death, bargain around Hood's Gate, and seem to oppose Shadow's certainty with uncertainty and chance.

characters.name: Gear
characters.summary: The Seventh Hound of Shadow. Gear tracks Hairlock through torn Warren paths into Pale, kills guards while hunting, attacks Tattersail's room, and is wounded by Paran before escaping back through Shadow.

characters.name: Hood's servant
characters.summary: A shifting apparition at Hood's Gate who speaks for the Lord of Death when Oponn interrupts Paran's passage. It accepts a bargain that another premature death will fall in Paran's shadow.

characters.name: Dassem Ultor
characters.summary: The former First Sword of the Empire, recalled in talk of the old wars and old wounds. Stories link him to Hood, the old Emperor's army, and a cascade of Ascendant interference, but he does not appear directly.

factions.name: 2nd Army
factions.summary: The Malazan army force around Pale under Dujek, including Tattersail and the Bridgeburners. Through Chapter Four it is under pressure from Tayschrenn, merger orders, and possible imperial house-cleaning.

factions.name: 5th Army
factions.summary: A battered Malazan army pushed hard in the North Campaign against Caladan Brood, the Tiste Andii, and the Crimson Guard.

factions.name: Bridgeburners
factions.summary: The battered elite unit under Dujek, now reduced after Pale. Whiskeyjack’s Ninth Squad is sent toward Darujhistan while old loyalties, mutiny fears, Sorry, and Paran’s command all strain the group.

factions.name: Claw
factions.summary: The imperial covert/assassin service. Topper commands at a high level; Kalam once belonged to it by strong implication; Toc the Younger is the last active Claw presence around the 2nd Army.

factions.name: Crimson Guard
factions.summary: Mercenaries allied with Caladan Brood and Tiste Andii forces against the Malazan 5th Army. They are mentioned but not shown directly.

factions.name: House Paran
factions.summary: A noble house from Unta. Ganoes Paran belongs to it, and Tavore Paran is also part of the household.

factions.name: Malazan Empire
factions.summary: The occupying imperial power in Itko Kan, ruled by Empress Laseen. Its soldiers, officers, and command structure frame the chapter's violence and investigation.

factions.name: Moranth Black
factions.summary: Black-armoured Moranth legions who enter Pale after the siege and receive an hour of slaughter against the city's people.

factions.name: Onearm's Host
factions.summary: A military command under High Fist Dujek Onearm, mentioned in connection with the Bridgeburners.

factions.name: Pale
factions.summary: A Free City under siege by the Malazan Empire. It falls in Chapter Two after the attack on Moon's Spawn and the Moranth entry into the city.

factions.name: Tiste Andii
factions.summary: A sorcerous people associated with Moon's Spawn and Anomander Rake. They are described as black-skinned, linked to Elder magic and Mother Dark, and dangerous to the Malazans.

factions.name: House Shadow
factions.summary: A newly active High House/Warren whose Hounds, Assassin, and King are now directly entangled with Sorry, Paran, Hairlock, and the Bridgeburners. Its motives remain hostile to Laseen but not safely understood.

factions.name: High House Death
factions.summary: A Deck of Dragons power focused around Hood, Death’s Gate, the Virgin, and the Mason. Through Chapter Four it touches Sorry, Paran, Tattersail’s readings, and the threat of a large barrow.

factions.name: Moranth
factions.summary: Armoured allied clans using colour identities and Quorl air transport. Green Moranth courier Paran toward Pale; Black Moranth transport troops and carry the Bridgeburners toward Darujhistan.

factions.name: Darujhistan
factions.summary: The last Free City and the next imperial target. Through Chapter Four it is mostly a destination and object of strategy: the Bridgeburners are being sent there covertly.

mysteries.question: Who is really moving against the Bridgeburners and Dujek?
mysteries.currentTheory: Tayschrenn is still the obvious suspect, but Chapters Three and Four keep open whether he acts for Laseen, himself, or within a larger imperial purge.

mysteries.question: What exactly is Sorry now?
mysteries.currentTheory: The squad strongly suspects a House Shadow connection, and the Chapter Four evidence is hard to ignore, but Whiskeyjack still insists on treating her as human until proven otherwise.

mysteries.question: What did Oponn do to Paran?
mysteries.currentTheory: Oponn kept Paran from passing fully through Hood’s Gate and returned him as a living piece in the game, but someone close to him may pay the death-price later.

mysteries.question: Can Hairlock be controlled?
mysteries.currentTheory: Quick Ben believes he still has a grip, but Hairlock claims otherwise, feeds on Chaos, threatens Tattersail, and may become as dangerous as the enemy he is tracking.

mysteries.question: What does the Mason of High House Death mean for Tattersail and the Bridgeburners?
mysteries.currentTheory: Tattersail reads a looming barrow and a great death pattern; by Chapter Four she fears the barrow may be large enough for all of them.

mysteries.question: Who or what is opposing Shadow?
mysteries.currentTheory: Oponn is visibly interfering, but the field also points toward High House Dark and a future clash with Shadow. The exact shape remains unknown.

relationships.summary: Rigga travels with the fishergirl and gives a prophecy before the road violence.
The fishergirl's unnamed father depends on her return and the repair of fishing nets.
Cotillion argues the fishergirl should not simply be discarded and chooses her for an unresolved purpose.
Ammanas discusses using the fishergirl in a way the Empress cannot trace.
They appear together after the slaughter and appear to share an agenda, though their identities remain unresolved.
Both are members of House Paran; Tavore is Ganoes Paran's younger sister.
Ganoes belongs to House Paran of Unta.
Tavore is part of the Paran household in Unta.
Garnet serves as a guard at House Paran and admits Ganoes.
Lorn takes Paran onto her staff after the massacre investigation.
Lorn serves as Adjunct to Empress Laseen.
Laseen is the Empress whose authority frames the imperial response.
The local captain reports to Lorn and is uneasy around the Adjunct.
Topper intercepts Paran by Warren; their exchange is civil but tense.
Topper is identified with the Claw, an imperial covert organization implied in the chapter.
Aragan questions the new soldier Sorry and records her attachment.
By the end of the chapter, Sorry is recorded as attached to the Bridgeburners.
The Bridgeburners are described under High Fist Dujek Onearm.
Onearm's Host is a military command under High Fist Dujek Onearm.
The fishergirl mentions Ilgrand Lender in connection with debt or repayment after fishing gear is lost.
Tattersail commands the surviving wizard cadre attached to the 2nd Army during the battle at Pale.
Calot is Tattersail’s cadre companion and intimate partner; he dies shielding her during the battle.
Hairlock and Tattersail serve in the same mage cadre, and after the battle he draws her into his suspicions.
Tattersail suspects Tayschrenn’s actions during the battle may have killed her comrades, but proof and motive remain unresolved.
Tayschrenn frames his decisions at Pale as Empress Laseen’s orders.
Nightchill is one of the High Mages Tayschrenn selects for the attack on Moon’s Spawn.
Bellurdan is one of the High Mages Tayschrenn selects for the attack on Moon’s Spawn.
A’Karonys is one of the High Mages Tayschrenn selects for the attack on Moon’s Spawn.
Nightchill and Bellurdan are described as lifelong companions.
Anomander Rake is named as Lord of the black-skinned Tiste Andii.
Rake is identified as the lord associated with Moon’s Spawn above Pale.

relationships.kind: allied / companion in report and poem
relationships.summary: The chapter links Rake and Caladan Brood through the war against the Empire and through old poetry, but their exact relationship remains known-so-far only.
Brood is mentioned alongside Tiste Andii and Crimson Guard forces opposing Malazan armies.
Whiskeyjack commands a surviving Bridgeburner squad after the disaster beneath Pale.
Quick Ben waits on Whiskeyjack’s approval before completing the dangerous rite around Hairlock.
Kalam is one of Whiskeyjack’s Bridgeburners and acts with Quick Ben during the post-battle rite.
Whiskeyjack treats Sorry as a recruit in his squad but is deeply unsettled by her presence.
Kalam protects the space around Quick Ben while Quick Ben works the lost rite on Hairlock.
Quick Ben shifts Hairlock’s soul into a marionette using a lost and dangerous art.
Hairlock believes Tayschrenn’s actions at Pale need answers and pulls Tattersail into that suspicion.
Fiddler and Hedge are mentioned together as sappers working on the collapsed tunnels.
Fiddler is mentioned as a Bridgeburner sapper.
Hedge is mentioned with Fiddler among the Bridgeburners.
Mallet is named by Whiskeyjack among the squad voices around him.
Trotts is named by Whiskeyjack among the squad voices around him.
Tattersail’s memory links Dancer and Mock to a past night of violence, but details remain unresolved.
Paran arrives in Pale as the new captain over Whiskeyjack’s Ninth Squad, a command nobody expects to be simple or safe.
Paran is sent to stay close to Sorry, and the shadow-linked attack on him appears tied to that mission.
Toc escorts Paran to Pale, warns him about army politics, and offers his name if trouble comes.
Toc says he is the last active Claw around the 2nd Army after others have been killed or exposed.
Toc belongs to the Second as an outrider and has the lived-in perspective of the army around Pale.
Tattersail finds Bellurdan mourning Nightchill’s remains and clashes with him over whether Tayschrenn betrayed them.
Tattersail agrees to stay in the Bridgeburners’ conspiracy after Whiskeyjack shares the truth about Sorry and Hairlock’s dual task.
Quick Ben needs Tattersail as protection around Hairlock, while Tattersail realizes his power is deeper than it should be for someone unknown to her.
Tattersail’s Deck readings and the Bridgeburners’ evidence make Sorry’s link to Shadow feel increasingly certain.
Quick Ben traces the Itko Kan massacre, Hounds, Lorn, and Sorry into one dark pattern.
Kalam is deeply wary of Sorry and reaches for a knife around her, but Whiskeyjack argues that may still mean human brutality rather than demonhood.
Whiskeyjack keeps Sorry in the squad and defends a human explanation, even while remembering why the Bridgeburners cannot accept her as one of their own.
Gear tracks Hairlock after Hairlock trespasses through Shadow and tears between Warrens using Chaos.
Quick Ben believes he is tracking and guiding Hairlock, but Hairlock claims he is already slipping that control.
Hairlock relies on Tattersail’s room and protection, then threatens her after she prevents him from taking Gear.
Gear crashes into Tattersail’s room while hunting Hairlock and nearly overwhelms her wards.
Paran unexpectedly pierces Gear’s magic with his sword, forcing the Hound to retreat.
Gear is the Seventh Hound among Shadowthrone’s servants and hunts the trespasser through Pale.

relationships.kind: chooses to follow rather than finish
relationships.summary: At Hood’s Gate, Shadowthrone decides Paran may be more useful alive as a trail to whoever opposes Shadow.
Oponn meddles with Paran’s passage through Hood’s Gate and returns him to life as a piece of chance.
Oponn bargains with Hood’s servant: Paran is spared, but another premature death will fall in his shadow.
Oponn’s meddling frustrates Shadow’s attempt to finish Paran and turns him into bait in a larger game.
Dujek and Whiskeyjack share old history and trust; Dujek gives him the Darujhistan orders and permission to walk if the squad survives.
Dujek promises to take care of the remaining Bridgeburners and lets Whiskeyjack’s squad walk if they live through Darujhistan.
Whiskeyjack listens when Fiddler’s instincts warn that something is about to be unleashed.
Picker discovers Paran’s body, notices the strange bleeding, and calls for a healer when he screams back to life.
Mallet heals what remains of Paran’s wounds and recognizes an outside force already repaired the fatal damage.
Hedge takes a liking to Paran after the attack, in the same stubborn way he adopts strays and needy creatures.
Tattersail’s spiral reading shows High House Dark’s Knight set against a Hound of Shadow, suggesting a future clash.

visualReferences.name: Warren doorway / threshold
visualReferences.summary: A spoiler-safe visual metaphor for a Warren as a dangerous threshold: part doorway, part hidden route through another layer of reality. Based only on early references to people arriving, intercepting, or travelling by Warren.
visualReferences.imageAlt: Spoiler-safe concept art of a Warren as a dark doorway-like threshold in the air
visualReferences.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache dark fantasy threshold view, spoiler-safe through Chapter Three

visualReferences.name: Quorl
visualReferences.summary: A spoiler-safe visual reference for the flying creatures used for overland travel. Known-so-far visual anchors: four wings, thin translucent membranes, and a dangerous sense of altitude.
visualReferences.imageAlt: Spoiler-safe concept art of a four-winged quorl flying high above rugged terrain
visualReferences.imageStyle: Painterly oil-and-gouache dark fantasy field-guide creature plate, spoiler-safe through Chapter Three
